H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (n=96, mean=7.10, std=1.82) leads over-stimulation protocols with strong efficacy and broad positive distribution. YouTube views (29) remain underwhelming despite high scores.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: One of the most effective for overstimulation, with many feeling better after about 10 minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (n=52, mean=6.83, std=1.84) offers reliable benefits with consistent distribution and qualitative alignment to L1. YouTube data is unavailable.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: A good alternative to Level 1 for calming an overwhelmed mind, working just as well.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68) maintains strong performance with varied settling times and useful sleep application. YouTube reach is limited.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Helps with sleep after feeling overwhelmed, often working within 5 minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93) shows strong efficacy with 63% positive responses. A bimodal distribution and qualitative feedback indicate reliable anxiety reduction. YouTube views are minimal (5), limiting reach analysis. AutoGluon alignment suggests stable patterns.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Many find this helpful for anxiety, with most noticing improvement within 10-20 minutes, especially when used alone or during therapy.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62) performs consistently with its L1 counterpart, showing slightly lower efficacy but similar reliability. Qualitative data confirms fast settling times, and YouTube reach remains limited.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This works well for anxiety too, sometimes faster than Level 1. The music feels less standout but still helps calm the mind.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89) matches series performance with a consistent distribution and varied settling times. YouTube data is unavailable, but confidence remains meaningful.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Another reliable anxiety option, useful whether you're using it for yourself or supporting someone else.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70) meets expectations for crisis contexts with lower mean scores. Qualitative reports show significant improvements despite challenges, and YouTube reach is minimal.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Helps in tough moments, though not as strongly as other protocols. Some report quick relief during crises.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (n=35, mean=6.40, std=1.72) aligns with crisis protocol performance, showing comparable efficacy to its L1 counterpart. Qualitative data highlights mixed music reception.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Another good option for acute distress, with mixed reactions to the music but consistent benefits.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91) completes the crisis series with consistent performance, showing immediate settling benefits and limited YouTube reach.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Can ease crisis moments immediately, completing the set of crisis support tools.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (n=55, mean=7.16, std=1.85) excels in sleep support with high scores and positive qualitative feedback. YouTube reach is minimal but efficacy is well-established.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: A dependable sleep aid that many find noticeably helpful, with pleasant music for relaxation.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55) matches L1 performance with caregiver applicability and mixed music reception. YouTube data is unavailable.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Works well for sleep, whether you're using it yourself or helping someone else unwind.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (n=29, mean=7.10, std=1.65) completes the sleep series with consistent results and bimodal response patterns. YouTube reach is minimal.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Completes the sleep tools effectively, matching earlier levels' steady results.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82) offers baseline focus support with mixed qualitative responses. YouTube reach is minimal.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: A basic focus aid, though some find the rhythm a bit too active.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60) outperforms L1 with strong focus benefits and qualitative confirmation. YouTube data is unavailable.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts concentration effectively, helping many focus better than Level 1.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (n=34, mean=7.56, std=1.96) leads the focus series with outstanding results and high YouTube views (15).
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Clearly the best for deep focus, making a real difference for many users.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (n=35, mean=6.74, std=1.70) provides baseline creative flow support with immediate settling benefits. YouTube views are minimal.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Provides mild but consistent creative flow support, sometimes kickstarting inspiration quickly.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (n=25, mean=6.72, std=1.43) aligns with L1 performance, showing stronger improvements and positive music reception. YouTube reach is limited.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Some find this more effective than Level 1 for creativity, with music many enjoy.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32) stands out with high efficacy and significant qualitative benefits. YouTube views (7) suggest potential for growth.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: The strongest creative flow tool yet, with several reporting significant improvements.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (n=26, mean=7.08, std=1.32) supports maintenance with long settling times and mixed qualitative feedback. YouTube reach is minimal.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance, though it may take 20 minutes to fully settle in.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03) shows lower efficacy than L1 with higher variance. Qualitative data highlights modest improvements.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Offers okay stability support but isn't as consistent as others.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.86, std=1.70) requires further validation with developing confidence and mixed settling reports. YouTube data is unavailable.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Shows potential but needs more data to confirm its effects.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (n=38, mean=6.68, std=1.93) supports mood enhancement with moderate improvements and limited YouTube reach.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: A good starting point for mood enhancement, lifting energy moderately.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (n=24, mean=6.71, std=1.73) aligns with L1 performance, showing reliable benefits and YouTube growth potential.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: More likely to boost mood and energy than Level 1.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (n=17, mean=6.24, std=1.79) requires evaluation due to mixed responses and limited YouTube reach.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Helps some but not all; needs more review to understand why.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (n=21, mean=6.24, std=1.97) offers modest motivation support with varied qualitative responses. YouTube reach is minimal.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Modestly helpful for motivation, with varied results.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (n=14, mean=6.00, std=1.57) underperforms with limited impact, requiring protocol refinement. YouTube data is unavailable.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Less effective for boosting drive, requiring refinement.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (n=12, mean=6.92, std=2.31) shows potential rebound effects with mixed qualitative feedback. YouTube reach is modest.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Shows promise for motivation but needs further evaluation.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (n=23, mean=6.65, std=1.58) provides solid cognitive support with positive qualitative reception. YouTube reach is modest.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Starts strong for mental clarity, with noticeable benefits for many.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (n=15, mean=6.60, std=1.45) aligns with L1 performance, showing consistent benefits and YouTube growth potential.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Follows up well, sometimes improving clarity even more than Level 1.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (n=9, mean=5.33, std=2.29) underperforms significantly, requiring redesign for better efficacy. Qualitative reports are mixed.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Not working as well as others; may need adjustments.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The HEALING series shows strong aggregate performance (mean=6.95, n=580), THRIVING matches this mean with lower variance, and TRANSFORMING trails as expected (mean=6.47).
• HEALING protocols demonstrate high consistency and reliability
• THRIVING protocols show the highest mean scores, particularly in Focus & Clarity
• TRANSFORMING's lower mean and higher variance align with its challenging design
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series reflects its design intent with distinct efficacy profiles.
• HEALING leads in listener consistency with low variance
• THRIVING's HOLD protocols show increasing efficacy with level
• TRANSFORMING's ASCENT protocols exhibit higher variance due to use in acute states
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show notable deviations from expected patterns.
• X-MC-L3 (n=9) has the lowest mean (5.33) in TRANSFORMING, warrants redesign
• T-FC-L3 (n=34) outperforms its series with mean=7.56
• H-CR-L1 and H-CR-L2 show lower means consistent with crisis contexts
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Key signals for protocol refinement emerge from the data.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 requires structural redesign to address low efficacy
• H-OS-L1 needs promotional prioritization due to high efficacy and low views
• T-FC-L3 suggests creating an expanded variant for focused work
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals evolving analytical understanding.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Notable drift in T-MB and H-SL protocols suggests unstable patterns
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Crisis protocols cluster tightly, validating their grouping
• Confidence in clusters is moderate due to recent shifts
